title: ZREVRANGEBYSCORE
description: Returns members in a sorted set within a range of scores in reverse order.
syntax: ZREVRANGEBYSCORE key max min [WITHSCORES] [LIMIT offset count]
complexity: O(log(N)+M) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set and M the number of elements being returned. If M is constant (e.g. always asking for the first 10 elements with LIMIT), you can consider it O(log(N)).

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility

content:
Returns all the elements in the sorted set at `key` with a score between `max` and `min` (including elements with score equal to `max` or `min`). In contrary to the default ordering of sorted sets, for this command the elements are considered to be ordered from high to low scores. The elements having the same score are returned in reverse lexicographical order. Apart from the reversed ordering, `ZREVRANGEBYSCORE` is similar to [`ZRANGEBYSCORE`](). ## Examples ZADD myzset 1 "one" ZADD myzset 2 "two" ZADD myzset 3 "three" ZREVRANGEBYSCORE myzset +inf -inf ZREVRANGEBYSCORE myzset 2 1 ZREVRANGEBYSCORE myzset 2 (1 ZREVRANGEBYSCORE myzset (2 (1 ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | Deprecated as of Redis v6.2.0. | ## Return information **RESP2:** * [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list of the members and, optionally, their scores in the specified score range. **RESP3:** * [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list of the members and, optionally, their scores in the specified score range.
intro:
Returns all the elements in the sorted set at `key` with a score between `max`
and `min` (including elements with score equal to `max` or `min`).
In contrary to the default ordering of sorted sets, for this command the
elements are considered to be ordered from high to low scores.

The elements having the same score are returned in reverse lexicographical
order.

Apart from the reversed ordering, `ZREVRANGEBYSCORE` is similar to
[`ZRANGEBYSCORE`]().

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Returns all the elements in the sorted set at `key` with a score between `max`
and `min` (including elements with score equal to `max` or `min`).
In contrary to the default ordering of sorted sets, for this command the
elements are considered to be ordered from high to low scores.

The elements having the same score are returned in reverse lexicographical
order.

Apart from the reversed ordering, `ZREVRANGEBYSCORE` is similar to
[`ZRANGEBYSCORE`]().

sections.title: Examples
sections.content:
ZADD myzset 1 "one"
ZADD myzset 2 "two"
ZADD myzset 3 "three"
ZREVRANGEBYSCORE myzset +inf -inf
ZREVRANGEBYSCORE myzset 2 1
ZREVRANGEBYSCORE myzset 2 (1
ZREVRANGEBYSCORE myzset (2 (1

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility
sections.content:
| Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | Deprecated as of Redis v6.2.0. |

sections.title: Return information
sections.content:
**RESP2:**

* [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list of the members and, optionally, their scores in the specified score range.

**RESP3:**

* [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list of the members and, optionally, their scores in the specified score range.
